title: adiantum incisum subsp. indicum
intro: Adiantum incisum subsp. indicum, also known as the Indian maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Adiantum incisum subsp. indicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
